title: Suraj S vs Inspector Of Police on 26 March, 2013
doc:
Suraj S vs Inspector Of Police on 26 March, 2013

Author: P.Bhavadasan

Bench: P.Bhavadasan

       

  

  

 
 
                            IN THE HIGH COURT OF KERALA AT ERNAKULAM

                                                        PRESENT:

                            THE HONOURABLE MR.JUSTICE P.BHAVADASAN

                  TUESDAY, THE 26TH DAY OF MARCH 2013/5TH CHAITHRA 1935

                                           Bail Appl..No. 2000 of 2013 ()
                                                -------------------------------
           CRIME NO. 415/2012 OF CYBER CRIME CELL, CENTRAL CRIME BRANCH,
                                        EGMORE, CHENNAI, TAMIL NADU
                                                    -----------------------

PETITIONER/ACCUSED :
------------------------------------

            SURAJ S., S/O.I.SASIDHARA KURUP,
            AGED 27 YEARS, ANANDAVILASOM,
            THATTA P.O., PATHANAMTHITTA - 691 525

            BY ADV. SMT.ASHA ELIZABETH MATHEW

RESPONDENTS/COMPLAINANT & S I OF POLICE :
------------------------------------------------------------------------

        1. INSPECTOR OF POLICE, CYBER CRIME CELL,
            CENTRAL CRIME BRANCH,
            EGMORE, CHENNAI - 220.

        2. SUB INSPECTOR OF POLICE,
            POLICE STATION, KODUMON
            PATHANAMTHITTA DISTRICT.

            R1 & R2 BY PUBLIC PROSECUTOR SRI. ROY THOMAS


            THIS BAIL APPLICATION HAVING COME UP FOR ADMISSION ON 26-03-2013,
            THE COURT ON THE SAME DAY PASSED THE FOLLOWING:


Mn
 


                      P.BHAVADASAN, J.
              ---------------------------------------
              Bail Application No.2000 OF 2013
              ---------------------------------------
            Dated this the 26th day of March, 2013.


                            O R D E R

The petitioner is accused in Crime No.415/2012 of Cyber Crime Cell, Central Crime Branch, Chennai who is alleged to have committed the offences punishable under Section 4 of the Women Harassment Act, 2000, Section 509 of the Indian Penal Code read with Section 66A of the Information Technology Act. The allegation against the petitioner is that he had taken the photographs of the de facto complainant, blackmailed her and in spite of the warning given by the Police, he published the photographs of her in the face book.

2. The petitioner points out that he has not done anything and he is totally innocent and has been falsely implicated in the matter. At any rate, according to him, he may be granted some time to surrender before the court concerned, and it is submitted that in the meanwhile, he may not be arrested. In support of the contention that this Court is competent to grant anticipatory bail, B.A.No.2000/2013 2 the learned counsel relied on the decision in Madhusoodan vs. Superintendent of Police (1992 KHC 301).

3. Learned Public Prosecutor opposed the petition and pointed out that the offences are of serious nature and the investigation is going on. Even in the complaint, a copy of which is produced along with the application, it is mentioned that the petitioner had been cautioned on two earlier occasions and still he continued his activities.

4. After having heard the learned counsel for the petitioner, the learned Public Prosecutor and after having perused the records, the claim of the petitioner that he is totally innocent may not be acceptable as of now. What are the nature of the materials collected by the investigating officer are not available to be considered by this Court at this stage. The crime has been registered in Tamil Nadu namely, in the Cyber Crime Cell and going by the records, First Information Report has been filed before the Chief Metropolitan Magistrate.

Even assuming that the petitioner is to be arrested within the jurisdiction of this Court, Section 81 of the Code of Criminal B.A.No.2000/2013 3 Procedure take care of the situation. There is no reason to believe that the investigating officer or the officer carrying out arrest will not act in accordance with law. This Court finds no ground to exercise its extra ordinary jurisdiction in favour of the petitioner. The application is devoid of merits and it is accordingly dismissed.

P.BHAVADASAN JUDGE smp
